zh.lifeSummary: 當Geoffrey de Warburton III於1298年出生在英國英格蘭爾瓦布爾頓時，他的父親Geoffery de Warburton26歲，母親Margaret le Boteler21歲。他於大約1327年與Alice De Wevers結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1382年在英國爾瓦布爾頓享年84歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Geoffrey de Warburton III于1298年出生在英国英格兰爾瓦布爾頓时，他的父亲Geoffery de Warburton26岁，母亲Margaret le Boteler21岁。他于大约1327年与Alice De Wevers结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1382年在英国爾瓦布爾頓享年84岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Warburton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from a place in Greater Manchester (formerly in Cheshire) called Warburton, from the Old English female personal name Wǣrburg (composed of the elements wǣr ‘pledge’ + burh ‘fortress’) + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Warbritton
meanings: Probably an altered form of English   Warburton  . This form of the surname is not found in English sources.

variants.name: Warbington
meanings: English (Lancashire): perhaps a variant of   Warburton  ; otherwise a habitational name from a lost or unidentified place.

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffrey de Warburton III was born in 1298, in Warburton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Geoffery de Warburton, was 26 and his mother, Margaret le Boteler, was 21. He married Alice De Wevers about 1327. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1382, in Warburton, Cheshire, England, at the age of 84.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Geoffrey de Warburton III est né en 1298, à Warburton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Geoffery de Warburton, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Margaret le Boteler, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Alice De Wevers environ 1327. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1382, à Warburton, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 84 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Geoffrey de Warburton III 1298 in Warburton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Geoffery de Warburton 26 und seine Mutter Margaret le Boteler 21. Er hat um 1327 Alice De Wevers geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1382 im Alter von 84 Jahren in Warburton, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey de Warburton III è nato il nel 1298 nel Warburton, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Geoffery de Warburton, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Margaret le Boteler, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Alice De Wevers circa 1327. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1382, nel Warburton, Cheshire, England, all'età di 84 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Warburton III(이)가 1298년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 워버턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Geoffery de Warburton(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret le Boteler(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1327년 Alice De Wevers(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1382년 영국 체실르 워버턴에서 84세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Alice De Wevers aproximadamente 1327. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1382, em Warburton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 84 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Geoffrey de Warburton III родился в 1298 г. в местности варбуртон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Geoffery de Warburton было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret le Boteler, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice De Wevers около 1327 г. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1382 г. в местности варбуртон, чешир, Англия в возрасте 84 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Geoffrey de Warburton III nació en 1298, en Warburton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Geoffery de Warburton, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Margaret le Boteler, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Alice De Wevers aproximadamente 1327. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1382, en Warburton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 84 años.
